name: Minneapolis Cooperative Network Activation — Federal Occupation
anti_capture_mechanisms: pre_existing_infrastructure: inter-cooperation agreements built before crisis, not under pressure
distributed_response: three independent cooperatives activated simultaneously without central coordination
deliberate_solidarity: infrastructure built deliberately, not desperately — papiris framing
embedded_mutual_aid: loan forbearance, childcare, documentation built into cooperative structure before need
cooperatives_involved: Shared Capital Cooperative (national CDFI loan fund)
services_activated: Loan forbearance — 3 months no repayment for all Minnesota borrowers (Shared Capital)
Community meals, coffee donations, baked goods, doors open for direct action participants (May Day Cafe)
Drop-in childcare during ICE Out protests, continued weekly on Fridays (Paper Moon)
outcome: Active. Infrastructure activated within 48 hours of federal occupation. Not improvisation — the relationships and agreements existed. Demonstrates Hampton's architecture in 2026: distribute mutual aid infrastructure before it's needed. Now in the permanent record.
source_verification_method: WebSearch confirmed USFWC blog post exists and details match. May Day Cafe became worker co-op February 7, 2026 (one year ownership anniversary). Shared Capital offered 3-month forbearance. Paper Moon offered drop-in childcare. Note: 'Living history documentation' previously listed in services was not verified in source — removed.
notes: The contemporary test case that made the framework operational. 'Not improvisation. Infrastructure that was already there.' Primary source: USFWC blog post confirmed. Details verified 2026-04-07.
